title: bridge-jmap: seen_ids ring buffer eviction can cause duplicate email delivery
description: bridge-jmap/src/bridge.rs SeenIds: the ring buffer is capped at 1000 entries. When the oldest id is evicted (after 1000 distinct emails seen), it becomes eligible for re-delivery if it still appears in the JMAP mailbox. The bridge uses a full-mailbox scan on every poll (email_query), so any evicted id that remains in the mailbox will be treated as new and forwarded to nie again. Comment says '1000 covers 20 full-scan cycles before first eviction' — but that is 20 cycles of PAGE_SIZE=50 new messages, not 20 total cycles. A mailbox with 1001+ persistent emails and new arrivals will hit this quickly. Consequence: duplicate messages forwarded to nie relay from email.
close_reason: seen_ids persisted to disk; bounded to last 1000 IDs; atomic write-then-rename; restart no longer causes re-delivery
